steps.title: Create one quadratic
steps.content:
At an intersection, the two expressions for $y$ are equal:
$$
x-c=-4(x-8)^2
$$
Move all terms to one side:
$$
4x^2-63x+256-c=0
$$

steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content:
Two distinct real solutions require a positive discriminant:
$$
(-63)^2-4(4)(256-c)>0
$$
$$
3969-4096+16c>0
$$
$$
16c>127
$$
$$
c>\frac{127}{16}
$$

steps.title: Pick a value above the boundary
steps.content:
Since
$$
\frac{127}{16}=7.9375
$$
the listed value greater than this boundary is $13$.

steps.title: Finish the comparison
steps.content: A value greater than the tangent boundary gives two distinct intersections between the line and the parabola. The answer is D.
